reviews.review_description: I got it when it was free so when it became payed I got it all for free and the levels are really fun

developer_response.body: Glad you're enjoying it, and thanks for your review!

reviews.review_description: I’ve really enjoyed this game. Took me a while to find the right sized space, but once I did it was fun. Very user friendly. Only the hand controls can be a little tricky at times. Picking things up and such. But not too often and I have enjoyed each episode

developer_response.body: Great to hear you're enjoying the game! Thanks very much for your review.

reviews.review_description: But more importantly and the response to my concerns were immediate. So it’s actually a 4 because of the hiccups I had, but you may not have, and the extra star is for the incredible customer service.

A great collection of 6 mysteries that require you to piece together what happened. It's fun interacting with the environment as you turn things over trying to find all the clues to help you work out what happens. It requires you to think beyond what you see in order to actually solve each case.

I attempted to download it. But can't play.

I am left the dark (spacial is activated but still, i am in a black void)
The only thing I see is a console with a login screen and 2 tools.

It does nothing else.
I deleted and reinstalled the app 3 times and it still does not work on my 3s...
I was looking to solve a mystery... but the mystery of the non working app was not what I was looking for...

developer_response.body: Hi, the game has just been updated to use the latest Mixed Reality Utility Kit, which I think will solve this issue. If you get the time, please give it another try.

reviews.review_description: It's fun. But you only get one free case, and the case isn't that long. The rest you have to pay for. It's not a lot but, still, bummer.

reviews.review_description: Fantastic game, looks just like a real crime scene in your house! Stunning visuals and excellent game mechanics. I only have the first level which is free but if the others are the same it will be money well spent. Would be a good idea to sell the others in a bundle at a discount if possible? Everyone should own this game.

reviews.review_title: A really charming detective puzzle game.
reviews.review_description: Loved this experience, a thought provoking narrative that each case made me think hard about. Fully supports both MR and VR modes with full locomotion.
Really love being able to navigate inside the crime scene by walking around. The gadgets are fun to use and the cases are well thought out

reviews.review_title: Love the game, just not enough cases yet
reviews.review_description: Love the game, especially when passthrough is an function within it. I bought all case files, but unfortunately there aren't that many yet. Concept is amazing and I love it, when further along and actually published in Meta app store this game rockets to the moon. Hope there are many more cases to come in the future. Best detective game for the quest platform on my opinion.
I know for a fact that my play area is 3 x 2 M because thats how big my mat is. Butt still it says my space is to small.

developer_response.body: Hi, this issue has been addressed in the latest update! You can now manually place the Mixed Reality play area when the game starts.

reviews.review_description: THERE IS THIS PART WERE THERE ARE RINGS ON MY HANDS AND THE FLOOTING THINGS AND MY VR ALLMOST DIED AND I WAS SO PISSED!!!!!!!!!!!!

developer_response.body: Hi Arthur, thanks for playing the game. If you are referring to the stutter that can occur the first time the Data Decryption puzzle appears, this has been addressed in the v1.1.2 update, please give that a go and see if it improves things for you!

reviews.review_title: 1. I love it 2. PLEASE REMOVE THIS
reviews.review_description:
1. I love these new pass through games and I love spy games and this game is pretty neato but 1 thing I have to say is with the newest update I keep on crashing whenever I start the game. I really wanted to buy the other levels but it’s not letting me do that. Hope you can fix that issue soon!

2. Please get rid of the 3D models they are ugly af. I liked it when they were just in my ear not staring at e with their big weird eyes. Game is great just work more on the models!

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks very much for your review! I really want to fix this crash bug - could you send an email to gamedev@davidaddis.com and we'll try to get to the bottom of it please?

I hear you about the 3D models, I'm considering my options about that!

reviews.review_description: Developer I try to download case 3 update and it won't let me download he keeps saying sorry you don't have permission to download this I don't know why it's saying that

developer_response.body: Thanks for reporting this issue! It's been resolved now, Case 3 (and 4) are fully working for purchase.

reviews.review_description:
Very cool concept but it needs alot more work.

first off it needs alot of play area,roughly 10x10.

2nd the items that it spawns tend to be too close to the boundary especially the floor so i had to raise that boundary up a couple clicks off the actual floor to get around that.

3rd the gadgets are very difficult to grab.

4th theres too much uninterruptable talking! 

5th once you have played a level, theres simply no  Replayability.

This game should drop the "mission" structure and go with  Procedually generated levels that utilize the gadgets and basic framework so that the game levels are always different! If not this game will suffer..

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your feedback, it's very useful to hear about which parts of the game do and don't work for you, I'll certainly keep them in mind during future development.

reviews.review_description: This game is so much fun, it's really immersive to play in passthrough using your own home as the crime scene, it's a really innovative way to play and it makes for a very compelling and engaging experience.
Only one free level

reviews.review_title: Breaks my heart it's not co op
reviews.review_description: Excellent, but gutted it's not co op
Interesting concept, nicely done.

The game does not use your spatial information from your room set up. What this means is that characters standing behind a chair or table make the furniture appear “see-through” rather than being occluded behind it. Also, for me all of the game items appeared in the middle of my kitchen counter, making it impossible to grab items or interact with the game elements. I don’t know if this was some sort of glitch, or if the camera is not designed to map onto real room layout like other MR games do.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for trying out the game! I've just fixed the glitch that would have made parts of the crime scene appear out of your play area. If you get a chance to download the update (v1.1.7a) and try again, it should work much better now - so thanks for bringing this to my attention.

Some kind of context-sensitive detection of furniture, and tailoring the crime scene to match it would have been very cool, but these features didn't exist when I started development and would be very complex to adapt into the game now. Definitely something I'll consider in future projects or a sequel, though.

reviews.review_description: Good idea, interesting concept, some problems related to usability of prompts, probably some tutorial references in first use BUT, before 5 minutes im start to feel dizzy, some motion problems a type of lag or something like that and that's a big issue when you are trying to solve a mystery. (quest 2, passthrough, level 1: introduction)

developer_response.body: Could you give me a bit of information about your setup please? Which headset are you using, are you playing with passthrough or in Pure VR mode? Did this happen on Level 1 (the introduction)? Thanks :)

developer_response.body: Hi, you can now manually place the Mixed Reality play area when you start the game. If this still doesn't let you access all areas, you can always turn on VR mode in the Options Menu and use locomotion. :)
